{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the information provided in the documents, enamel has a complex structure with hydroxyapatite crystals forming enamel rods. The inner enamel layer has a decussating pattern that creates Hunter-Schreger bands, which account for its resistance to crack formation. This suggests that the inner part of the enamel, which would be closer to the dentin (tail), is more resistant to dissolution compared to the outer part (head). Therefore, the head is less resistant to dissolution.", "answer_choice": "C"}
